bbc.title: US launches strikes on Iran after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
bbc.description: The US says the strikes are intended to impose "heavy costs", while Iran warns it will "take decisive measures" in response.

bbc.title: Marine Le Pen to run for French presidency and appeal conviction in top court
bbc.description: The hard-right National Rally leader has ended months of speculation, after a court ruled she could run but would have to wear a tag for a year.

bbc.title: Trump looms large as Nato grapples with challenge of rearming Europe
bbc.description: The US president's inflammatory words have punctuated the image of unity at this crucial gathering, Security Correspondent Frank Gardner writes.

bbc.title: Woman suspected of Monaco bomb attack found dead in Ukraine
bbc.description: Police had been hunting Anastasiia Berezovska after a Ukrainian millionaire was injured in the blast.

bbc.title: Lawyer says detained Gaza doctor was severely beaten in Israeli jail
bbc.description: The lawyer for Dr Hussam Abu Safiya tells the BBC his client was so badly beaten he could not recognise him during a visit last week.

bbc.title: New footage shown at Kirk hearing shows man on rooftop near shooting scene
bbc.description: The preliminary hearing will determine whether Tyler Robinson, accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, will stand trial.

bbc.title: Ghana halts visit by South African president amid row over anti-migrant protests
bbc.description: Many in Ghana feared his presence would lead to mass protests, and officials say it is postponed for now.

bbc.title: Hungary's public news broadcasts halted in bid to scrap Orban-era propaganda
bbc.description: The country's main state TV channel displayed a message saying it was "sorry" for lying on Tuesday.

bbc.title: One million more UK homeowners set  to face higher mortgages
bbc.description: Homeowners face paying £45 a month more on average when they move onto a new deal over the next two years.

bbc.title: Online gamblers betting more than £1,000 to face new checks
bbc.description: Punters who bet more than £1,000 online in a 24-hour window will have to undergo an assessment, the regulator says.

bbc.title: Banks accused of pushing customers away from basic accounts
bbc.description: Some of the UK's biggest banks have been failing their most vulnerable customers, according to the financial regulator.

bbc.title: Amazon bars breastfeeding boss from business course
bbc.description: Rachael Bews says she told on the way her child would not be allowed on site. Amazon has apologised for not communicating its policy clearly.

bbc.title: 'I wear it on my middle finger': The rise of the defiant divorce ring
bbc.description: Around the world women are marking their split with new - or repurposed engagement - rings.

bbc.title: Robots available for rent: But what can they do?
bbc.description: Robotics tech is changing fast, so for many it makes sense to rent a robot.

bbc.title: Samsung profits jump 1,800% as AI chip sales soar
bbc.description: The firm's shares fell sharply on Tuesday as some investors had expected an even stronger performance.

bbc.title: Phone contract comparisons 'amounted to mis-selling' student loans, MPs say
bbc.description: A new report says students were not well-enough informed that their loan terms could change retrospectively.
